subject: [RFC PATCH 36/40] mm: page_alloc: set ALLOC_NOFRAGMENT on alloc_frozen_pages_nolock_noprof

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch adds the ALLOC_NOFRAGMENT flag to the alloc_frozen_pages_nolock_noprof function in the Linux kernel's page allocator. This flag steers the allocation towards tainted-SPB sub-pageblock space before fragmenting clean superpageblocks, which helps prevent atomic kmalloc_nolock callers from tainting hugepages unnecessarily. The patch addresses a performance issue where these callers would hit the fallback path on every PCP miss and convert movable pageblocks to UNMOVABLE. The flag is purely a steering hint and does not introduce any sleeping locks or depend on __GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM, making it safe for use in various contexts such as NMI, hardirq, and PREEMPT_RT.
